Footer.tagline: With Assoc. Prof. Dr. Doğukan Atabay, trusted care in Trabzon for interventional radiology, non-surgical varicose and thyroid nodule treatment, biopsy and diagnostics. Imperial Hospital – Trabzon.

slides.title:

slides.desc: Foam, laser and radiofrequency for comfortable, incision-free treatment. Same-day discharge.

HomePage.howBody: After examination and colour Doppler ultrasound we choose the best treatment for you. Laser, foam or radiofrequency procedures are often performed without surgery, with same-day discharge.
HomePage.aboutP1: I was born in Iğdır in 1985. I completed primary and secondary school in Iğdır. I started medical school at Atatürk University in 2003 and graduated as a physician in 2009. Between 2010 and 2015 I completed my radiology residency at Karadeniz Technical University Faculty of Medicine. My thesis focused on mid- and long-term follow-up of cerebral aneurysms treated endovascularly.
HomePage.aboutP2: I completed my compulsory public service as a specialist at Trabzon Kanuni Training and Research Hospital (2015–2020). During residency I completed basic and advanced interventional radiology courses and obtained my interventional radiology diploma. I focused on interventional procedures throughout residency and compulsory service.
HomePage.aboutP3: I am a member of the Turkish Medical Association, Turkish Society of Radiology and Turkish Society of Interventional Radiology. Today I see patients at Imperial Hospital – Trabzon for interventional radiology, non-surgical varicose and thyroid nodule treatment, and diagnostic procedures.
HomePage.servicesDesc: Expert team for interventional radiology, varicose vein treatment and diagnostic procedures.

AboutPage.title: About
AboutPage.description: Assoc. Prof. Dr. Doğukan Atabay – interventional radiologist, training and experience. Imperial Hospital Trabzon.
AboutPage.heroDesc: Assoc. Prof. Dr. Doğukan Atabay — interventional radiologist.
AboutPage.aboutP1: I was born in Iğdır in 1985. I completed primary and secondary school in Iğdır. I started medical school at Atatürk University in 2003 and graduated as a physician in 2009. Between 2010 and 2015 I completed my radiology residency at Karadeniz Technical University Faculty of Medicine. My thesis focused on mid- and long-term follow-up of cerebral aneurysms treated endovascularly.
AboutPage.aboutP2: I completed my compulsory public service as a specialist at Trabzon Kanuni Training and Research Hospital (2015–2020). During residency I completed basic and advanced interventional radiology courses and obtained my interventional radiology diploma. I focused on interventional procedures throughout residency and compulsory service.
AboutPage.aboutP3: I am a member of the Turkish Medical Association, Turkish Society of Radiology and Turkish Society of Interventional Radiology. Today I see patients at Imperial Hospital – Trabzon for interventional radiology, non-surgical varicose and thyroid nodule treatment, and diagnostic procedures.
AboutPage.hospitalLead: Since 2007, Private Imperial Hospital has been one of the leading healthcare institutions in the Black Sea region. With a specialist physician staff and modern diagnostic and treatment units, it provides uninterrupted 24/7 care. The hospital has more than 30 medical departments, modern operating theatres, an expert anaesthesia team and a continuous emergency department. With online appointments and e-results, Imperial offers a broad range of services including surgery, internal medicine, radiology and interventional radiology.
AboutPage.hospitalAddress: Kemerkaya Mah., Devlet Coastal Road, Imperial Hospital No: 5, Ortahisar / Trabzon
AboutPage.feat1Desc: Modern theatres and expert anaesthesia team for open and laparoscopic procedures.
AboutPage.feat2Desc: Continuous emergency service so the hospital is always reachable.
AboutPage.feat3Desc: Appointments by web or phone and online result tracking.
AboutPage.feat4Desc: Broad spectrum including surgery, internal medicine, radiology and diagnostic imaging.
AboutPage.depsIntro: Comprehensive care with surgical, internal medicine and diagnostic units.

ServiceDetail.sidebarLead: If you have questions about this service or would like to book an appointment, please contact us.

BlogDetail.sidebarLead: If you have questions about this topic or our other services, please contact us.
BlogDetail.faq0q: Can I book an appointment about this topic?
BlogDetail.faq0a: Yes. For appointments or information about the procedures mentioned in this article—or similar topics—you can call us or use the contact form.
BlogDetail.faq1q: Is the information in this article up to date?
BlogDetail.faq1a: Our blog posts are for general information only. A personal diagnosis and treatment plan always require an examination and appropriate imaging.
BlogDetail.faq2q: How do I book an appointment?
BlogDetail.faq2a: You can reach us by phone or WhatsApp, or fill out the form on our contact page. We will get back to you as soon as possible.
BlogDetail.faq3q: Can I get information about fees?
BlogDetail.faq3a: You can get detailed information about procedure and consultation fees by phone when you book or beforehand.
BlogDetail.faq4q: Do you work with insurance providers?
BlogDetail.faq4a: You can contact our phone number for information on contracted insurers and payment options.
BlogDetail.faq5q: Do I need to prepare before a procedure?
BlogDetail.faq5a: Depending on the procedure, fasting, pausing certain medications, or other steps may be needed. You will receive personalized instructions when you book.
BlogDetail.faq6a: Monday–Friday 08:00–17:00, Saturday 08:00–13:00. Current hours are confirmed when you book.
BlogDetail.faq7a: We serve patients at İller Sk. 27-29, Kemerkaya, Imperial Hospital – Ortahisar, Trabzon.
